headline: GOLD HOLDS $4,700 AS STRAIT OF HORMUZ STANDOFF STALLS PEACE TALKS; WEEKLY SLIDE DESPITE 42% YEAR-ON-YEAR GAIN
snippet: Spot gold held above $4,700/oz on April 24-25 as limited progress in Strait of Hormuz peace negotiations weighed on sentiment. Silver ended the week below $76/oz. Despite the weekly pullback, gold is up 41.85% year-on-year and analysts see any dip as a buying opportunity.

headline: MINERS BREAK OUT: GDX SURGES 6.16%, SIL RALLIES 6.56% AS GOLD AND SILVER CLEAR KEY RESISTANCE ZONES
snippet: Gold jumped 2.8% and silver surged 5.38% in a single trading session, pushing GDX above the $42-$46 multi-year resistance zone and SIL through $38-$43 resistance. Analysts say precious metal miners are finally catching up to the metal's gains — a classic late-cycle breakout signal.

headline: COT REPORT APRIL 24: GOLD, SILVER AND USDX SPECULATIVE POSITIONING AS BULLS HOLD NEAR RECORD LEVELS
snippet: The weekly Commitments of Traders data for gold, silver, and the US Dollar index covers how large speculators and commercial hedgers are positioned as gold consolidates near all-time highs. Required reading for anyone tracking institutional flows in precious metals.

headline: CONTANGO SILVER & GOLD COMMITS $63M TO ALASKA AND BC EXPLORATION — $25M EARMARKED FOR KITSAULT VALLEY
snippet: Contango Silver & Gold Inc. unveiled a $63M exploration budget across its consolidated Alaska and Northern BC portfolio, with the largest single allocation of $25M going to Kitsault Valley for 40,000 metres of infill drilling — one of the most aggressive junior exploration commitments announced in 2026.

headline: KAMLOOPS JUNIOR GOLD 2026: EXPLORATION DRILLING UPDATE AND NEW ANALYST PRICE TARGETS ON THE CHARTS
snippet: An April 24 analyst update on a BC junior gold explorer covering recent drilling results and revised price targets as the gold bull market drives re-rating of early-stage assets. Note: Streetwise Reports may have commercial relationships with featured companies.

headline: COPPER SLIDES 1.6% TO $5.98/LB ON HORMUZ UNCERTAINTY; US TARIFF DECISION ON REFINED IMPORTS DUE MID-2026
snippet: Copper fell to $5.98/lb on April 24 — down 1.57% on the day — as risk-off sentiment from stalled Strait of Hormuz negotiations weighed on base metals. The US is expected to announce a 15% tariff on refined copper imports by mid-2026, driving strategic stockpiling by American manufacturers. China's pre-Labour Day restocking provided limited support.

headline: USA RARE EARTH SEALS $2.8B DEAL FOR BRAZIL'S SERRA VERDE TO CHALLENGE CHINA'S RARE EARTH DOMINANCE
snippet: USA Rare Earth agreed to acquire Brazil's Serra Verde — the only operating rare earth mine in the Americas — for $2.8B in cash and stock, adding 6,400 metric tonnes of annual rare earth oxide capacity including more than half of all heavy rare earths produced outside China. The CEO says more acquisitions are planned across the mining-to-magnet supply chain.

headline: IS CRITICAL METALS (CRML) STILL A BUY AFTER A 23% SURGE ON ITS GREENLAND TANBREEZ STAKE WIN?
snippet: The Motley Fool weighs the investment case for Critical Metals Corp. (CRML) after Greenland approved its 92.5% stake in the Tanbreez heavy rare earth deposit. Pilot plant operations are targeted for May 2026 with all eight critical heavy rare earth elements present on-site, but the stock surged 23.6% on the news — so timing matters.

headline: REUTERS POLL: FED RATE CUT PUSHED BACK TO LATE 2026 AS HORMUZ BLOCKADE FUELS WAR-RELATED INFLATION
snippet: A Reuters poll of economists finds the Federal Reserve will delay rate cuts until late 2026, as the ongoing Strait of Hormuz closure drives energy prices and inflation expectations higher. Higher-for-longer rates are a near-term headwind for non-yielding gold, but rising real inflation is a competing tailwind that has historically benefited the metal.

headline: SILVER DEFIES BUST: WHY THE METAL REFUSES TO CRATER AFTER ITS 149% PARABOLIC SURGE
snippet: After an astounding 149% rally in just over three months through late January 2026, silver has defied the classic post-parabolic bust pattern. The author argues that Chinese industrial demand — especially from solar manufacturing — and ongoing Western supply constraints are providing a structural floor. Bank of America has a 2026 silver target of $135 to $309/oz.

headline: GOLD DIP BUYERS RETURN: BIDS EMERGE BELOW $4,700 AS WEEKLY PULLBACK MEETS SUPPORT
snippet: After gold pulled back from its recent highs, dip-buyers re-emerged near the $4,700 level, suggesting underlying demand remains intact despite geopolitical headwinds. The author frames the weekly decline as healthy consolidation in a structural bull market rather than a trend reversal.
